response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with no demonstrated background in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, deep learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. The resume centers on Node.js, TypeScript, Ember, and D-language web/GIS work, which does not align with a Principal Performance Architect role for AI accelerators. There is also no PhD/Master's in a relevant hardware discipline and no indication of SIMD/MIMD or DNN training/inference experience.
strengths: Long software engineering tenure with exposure to low-latency systems (Sociomantic) and some C/C++ at National Instruments
Bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis suggests basic signal-processing/DSP familiarity
Comfort with multiple languages and platforms, including embedded mentions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or AI accelerator experience
No deep learning, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD workload optimization background
Resume career timeline is inconsistent and overlapping, raising credibility questions

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture expertise
Some low-level C/C++ and DSP exposure
